content:
Member-only story

5 min read
Just now

--

Learn about Distributed Training in TensorFlow. Explore the basics of parallel computing and distributed strategies for training TensorFlow models.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

I’ve often noticed that many people rush into training models without fully understanding and leveraging the framework of use. It’s the same old pipeline used everywhere irrespective of the complexity of the data and the hardware in use. Sure, you’ll end up with trained models but you’ll also have to deal with an unnecessarily inflated hardware cost which is perfectly avoidable if you understand the capabilities of the hardware and the framework in use for the model.
The previous parts cover a bunch of strategies you can use to train your models faster without impact precision but none of them are directly related to hardware directly. This blog deals with Distribution Strategy, where we will understand how to make the best out of your hardware.
Since the scope of all the topics being covered is limited to GPUs, we will continue with that trend and focus explicitly on data parallelism.
Before we begin, let’s discuss some of the common terms used in parallel computing.
